raw_consumer_statement: My husband and I went to XXXX XXXX on our honeymoon, upon arriving at the airport, we took a taxi service to our all-inclusive resort which was 20 minutes from the airport. The taxi driver quoted a price of US {$45.00} which we accepted. Upon arriving to the hotel, the driver showed us the price entered into the merchant device and we provided our Citi American Airlines credit card. Upon checking my credit card statement I saw that we were charged US {$640.00} instead of US {$45.00}. I filed a dispute with Citibank and provided our flight information our hotel information and a screenshot of the distance from the airport to our hotel, my dispute was denied two times by Citibank. Why would I pay XXXX {$640.00} for a XXXX minute ride from the airport to our hotel. We took a taxi from the hotel back to the airport, and I took a screenshot of the charge and provided that to Citibank as well too. This is a taxi service that does not provide a receipt. They quote you a price and you pay that price and Citibank is asking for an invoice or a receipt which I can not provide but common sense would state that a taxi does not cost US {$640.00} especially when I provided proof of the second taxi ride, which charged the right amount of {$45.00}.
